summary: Client website build prompts must prevent public AI/process wording, placeholder pages, internal headings, and invented proof; every public customer route needs a forbidden-phrase scan and customer-facing read-through before handoff.
core_insight: Visitors judge credibility from visible copy. Internal planning phrases, SEO notes, placeholder examples, and invented proof make a client website feel AI-generated or template-filled even when the implementation is technically correct.
prevention: Generated prompts should require natural business English, verified facts only, public placeholder removal/404 behavior, factual image captions, route-level forbidden phrase scans, and handoff counts for remaining process wording and public placeholders.

summary: Client-site handoff readiness must validate every visitor-facing surface locally and in production, not just page body copy.
core_insight: Launch risk often hides in metadata, schema, public API responses, form fallbacks, deleted demo URLs, navigation, sample articles, and handoff reports. These are not client-dependent issues; the build agent should fix them directly before handoff.
prevention: Generated prompts should preserve approved design/SEO/navigation unless a specific issue requires change, remove sample/fake/setup content, verify deleted sample URLs, validate production URL behavior, use one stable report filename with Report History, and provide issue-level before/after evidence.
